applied_optimizations.name: Triton kernel for spatial mean subtraction
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented Triton kernel for elementwise spatial mean subtraction. Uses PyTorch for ConvTranspose3d and BatchNorm3d (both already optimized), and Triton for the final mean subtraction operation. Single kernel launch for all elements.

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile with Triton kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Import error with torch._higher_order_ops.triton_kernel_wrap trying to import specialize_impl from triton.runtime.jit

attempted_but_failed.name: Fused BatchNorm + spatial mean in Triton with loop accumulation
attempted_but_failed.reason: Loop-based accumulation in Triton kernel produced incorrect results - accumulation across loop iterations didn't work correctly

attempted_but_failed.name: Per-slice Triton kernel launches
attempted_but_failed.reason: Python loop overhead for 512 kernel launches (16 batches * 32 channels) caused 10x slowdown

notes: Critical finding: Must NOT call batch_norm.eval() in forward - reference Model runs BatchNorm in training mode
Test harness relies on seeding (set_seed) for weight matching, not state_dict copying
Current implementation achieves correctness with 1.55ms runtime vs 1.45ms reference (0.94x speedup)
Further optimization opportunities: fuse mean computation into Triton kernel, reduce memory copies
